content.question: Surprisingly to most people, postal services have ___ in some parts of the world for thousands of years.
There is some ___ that a postal service existed among the Assyrians and Babylonians.
___ China a regular postal service was established in the 7th century B.C...
...a regular postal service was ___ in the 7th century B.C...
...and over the centuries attained such a high ___ of efficiency...
...that about 2,000 years after ___ institution it won the admiration...
...it ___ the admiration of travellers such as Marco Polo.
...the admiration of travellers ___ as Marco Polo.
Efficient and highly developed postal services were ___ established in the Persian and Roman empires.
These services in ___ times were mainly confined to the use of the state...
...mainly confined ___ the use of the state...
...private citizens made ___ of slaves, the couriers of other countries...
...to ___ messages and documents.
In Medieval Europe, postal services were organised by emperors and by the papacy, ___ private citizens continued to entrust their correspondence to others.
...private citizens continued to entrust their correspondence to ___ kinds of travellers.
Later, towards the 13th century, the universities and towns came to have their ___ messengers.
It was not ___ the 14th century that merchants began to set up regular courier services.
...merchants, the private citizens who had the greatest ___ of a speedy and regular exchange of correspondence...
...began to set ___ regular courier services.
The needs of business ___ to the organising of the postal service as we know it today.
